Number of Arms Included.value: Patients were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: darolutamide or placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Quality of Life Scale.value: What a measure of the participants' overall well-being and satisfaction with their health status, as assessed through a standardized questionnaire or survey, reflecting their perceived quality of life during the clinical trial?.

Metastases - N (%) | Bone | Treatment.value: Most had bone metastases in high-volume disease. High-volume is defined as visceral metastases and/or ≥ 4 bone metastases with ≥ 1 beyond the vertebral column/pelvis.

Metastases - N (%) | Bone | Control.value: Most had bone metastases in high-volume disease. High-volume is defined as visceral metastases and/or ≥ 4 bone metastases with ≥ 1 beyond the vertebral column/pelvis.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: Darolutamide prolonged time to castration resistance compared with placebo in patients with high-volume (HR, 0.41; 95% CI, 0.34 to 0.49) and low-volume metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer (HR, 0.21; 95% CI, 0.14 to 0.33) as well as in those with high-risk (HR, 0.38; 95% CI, 0.32 to 0.46) and low-risk metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer (HR, 0.32; 95% CI, 0.23 to 0.45; Fig 3). The effects of darolutamide on other key clinically relevant secondary efficacy end points are shown in Table 2.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.value: Darolutamide group had a significant reduction in the risk of death by 32.5% (HR, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.57 to 0.80; P<.0001) compared with the placebo group.

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Control.value: Median survival was 42.4 months (95% CI, 39.7 to 46.0 months)

Treatment Class.value: darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel

Type of Therapy.value: darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel
